2606.31722.title: Adapting Foundation ASR Models to Dysarthric Speech: A Case Study
2606.31722.paper_summary: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems often perform poorly in dysarthric speech, limiting their usefulness to affected speakers in everyday communication. This paper presents a personalized ASR system for a dysarthric speaker, built by adapting a foundation ASR model to speaker-specific data. Using the TEQST tool, we collected 92 hours of read speech and later added 8.8 hours of user corrections gathered through a deployed mobile application. Starting from Whisper, fine-tuning reduced word error rate to 15.8% with only 1.4 hours of adaptation data, reached 10.7% with 22.5 hours, and achieved the best result of 9.7% when using all available data including the corrections. Using LoRA adaptation and/or Qwen3-ASR as foundation model performed worse in this setting. The results show that personalized fine-tuning can make foundation ASR models substantially more effective for dysarthric speech and suitable for practical deployment.
2606.31722.paper_summary_zh: 自動語音辨識（ASR）系統在口齒不清的語音中表現通常不佳，限制了其在日常交流中對受影響講者的實用性。本文提出了一種針對口齒不清講者的個性化 ASR 系統，通過將基礎 ASR 模型調整為特定講者的數據來構建。使用 TEQST 工具，我們收集了 92 小時的朗讀語音，並在後來通過已部署的移動應用程序添加了 8.8 小時的用戶修正。從 Whisper 開始，微調將字詞錯誤率降低至 15.8%，僅使用 1.4 小時的適應數據，使用 22.5 小時達到 10.7%，並在使用所有可用數據（包括修正）時達到最佳結果 9.7%。在這種設置中，使用 LoRA 調整和/或 Qwen3-ASR 作為基礎模型的表現較差。結果顯示，個性化微調可以使基礎 ASR 模型對口齒不清的語音變得更有效，並適合實際部署。
2606.31722.author: Christian Huber et.al.
2606.31722.authors: Christian Huber, Laura Kernahan, Alexander Waibel
